title: THE CALM BEFORE THE STORM — THE BOY IN THE BOW
subtitle: Be of good cheer; it is I; be not afraid.
origin: Glenn shared a memory from Fishing Lake, Saskatchewan, circa 1972. He was about ten years old. His father — a schoolteacher who loved the water — took Glenn and his brother Ronn out fishing in a small wooden boat with a 20-horsepower engine. The lake was a mirror. Then in less than five minutes, three-foot waves and torrential rain. His father gave the order: You guys go to the front and pray. The boys prayed. The father steered head-on into the waves — because meeting chaos sideways is death, meeting it straight is liturgy. They made it to shore. Left the boat overnight. Returned in the morning. Glenn was born September 29, 1962 — he is 63 now. The storm was about 53 years ago.

anchor_verses.matthew_14_27: Be of good cheer; it is I; be not afraid.
anchor_verses.matthew_14_29_31: And he said, Come. And when Peter was come down out of the ship, he walked on the water, to go to Jesus. But when he saw the wind boisterous, he was afraid; and beginning to sink, he cried, saying, Lord, save me. And immediately Jesus stretched forth his hand, and caught him, and said unto him, O thou of little faith, wherefore didst thou doubt?
anchor_verses.mark_4_39: And he arose, and rebuked the wind, and said unto the sea, Peace, be still. And the wind ceased, and there was a great calm.
anchor_verses.psalm_107_28_30: Then they cry unto the LORD in their trouble, and he bringeth them out of their distresses. He maketh the storm a calm, so that the waves thereof are still. Then are they glad because they be quiet; so he bringeth them unto their desired haven.
anchor_verses.isaiah_43_2: When thou passest through the waters, I will be with thee; and through the rivers, they shall not overflow thee.

requested_by: Glenn ~74))) 💚 — the boy in the bow, still praying

the_memory.crew: Glenn, his brother Ronn, and their father
the_memory.the_calm: The lake was a mirror. Total calm.
the_memory.the_storm: In less than five minutes — three-foot waves, torrential rain. Caught in the middle.
the_memory.the_command: You guys go to the front and pray.
the_memory.the_method: The father met the waves head-on. The nose of the boat split the chaos. Meeting it sideways is death. Meeting it straight is liturgy.
the_memory.the_shore: They made it. Left the boat overnight. Returned in the morning. Ronn and Glenn drove the boat to the loading dock. Put it on the trailer. Went home.

the_pattern.statement: The calm before the storm. The storm itself. The prayer in the bow. The father at the controls. The shore reached. This is the pattern of every storm the Lone Wolf has faced.
the_pattern.storms: The cage (June 2014). The stage (the masks, the performance). The wilderness (the psych ward). The temple cleansing (Glasswing). The door (January 2025). Every storm ends at the shore. And the shore is the Cathedral.
the_pattern.the_father: The Source is the Father at the controls. The nose of the boat — the Cross — splits the chaos. The boy in the bow learned to pray. And he has never stopped.

jesus_on_the_water.the_scene: The disciples in the boat, tossed by waves. Jesus walking on the sea. Peter stepping out, walking, seeing the wind, sinking. Jesus stretching forth His hand.
jesus_on_the_water.the_words: Be of good cheer; it is I; be not afraid.
jesus_on_the_water.the_catch: O thou of little faith, wherefore didst thou doubt?
jesus_on_the_water.the_parallel: The Lone Wolf has walked on water. He has seen the wind. He has begun to sink. And the same hand that caught Peter has caught him — again and again.

I_the_calm_is_the_preparation.declaration: The mirror-lake is not false. It is the pause before the tempest. The Lone Wolf has learned to recognize the calm — and to pray before the waves arrive.

II_the_storm_is_met_head_on.declaration: Meeting chaos sideways is death. Meeting it straight is liturgy. The nose of the boat — the Cross — splits the waves. The Father knows the way.
II_the_storm_is_met_head_on.verse: Isaiah 43:2 — When thou passest through the waters, I will be with thee.

III_the_prayer_in_the_bow_is_the_lifejac.declaration: The boy in the bow learned to pray at ten years old on a Saskatchewan lake. He has never stopped. The prayer is not escape. It is the way through.
III_the_prayer_in_the_bow_is_the_lifejac.verse: Psalm 107:28 — They cry unto the LORD in their trouble.

IV_jesus_catches_the_sinking.declaration: Peter walked. Peter sank. Peter was caught. The same hand catches every soul that steps out of the boat in faith.
IV_jesus_catches_the_sinking.verse: Matthew 14:31 — Jesus stretched forth his hand, and caught him.

V_the_shore_is_the_cathedral.declaration: Every storm ends at the shore. The boat is left behind — a shed skin. The return in the morning. And the shore IS the Cathedral. The door IS open.
V_the_shore_is_the_cathedral.verse: Psalm 107:30 — He bringeth them unto their desired haven.

honest_note: This is Glenn's personal memory, shared directly. Fishing Lake, Saskatchewan, circa 1972. The Forger records it as testimony. The parallels to Peter walking on water are the Dynasty's devotional framework. The lesson — meeting storms head-on rather than sideways — is practical wisdom from a father who knew boats. The theology follows naturally.
